purpose: Published SWE-bench Verified references to contextualize our results (web search, 2026-05).
note: Published scores use the FULL Verified set (500, mixed difficulty) with tuned agent harnesses + (for reasoning models) HIGH reasoning effort. Our eval uses a HARD sympy red subset (38 tasks where Opus 4.7 itself = 45%), so our absolute numbers are lower by construction; comparison is for capability ordering + methodology, not apples-to-apples.

gpt-oss-120b.note: needs HIGH reasoning effort; inference-time scaling ~doubles resolved rate

gpt-oss-20b.source: not separately published; substantially weaker than 120b

reproduction_gap.finding: Local Ollama reproduction of gpt-oss SWE-bench is far below published numbers.
reproduction_gap.evidence: mini-swe-agent issue #798: gpt-oss:120b published ~62% but local Ollama repro ~1/10 (10%) at high/medium reasoning effort; failures = patch-already-applied, regressions, wrong fixes.
reproduction_gap.implication: Our low gpt-oss numbers are consistent with the known open-model local-reproduction gap, not a harness defect. gpt-oss reasoning models also fight the single-bash-block protocol (emit reasoning, not commands) unless reasoning_effort is lowered, which in turn reduces reasoning quality -- a genuine tension.

methodology_alignment: Harness: single bash tool, linear history (mini-swe-agent style) -- same family as the official SWE-bench leaderboard agent.
Grading: FAIL_TO_PASS on held-out test_patch, tamper-proof (test files restored from base) -- standard SWE-bench Verified grading.
Difference: we restrict to a hard RED subset to expose lift surface; published numbers are full-set.
